daily_brief: Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz escalated sharply Monday as the U.S. launched 'Project Freedom' to escort stranded commercial vessels through the waterway, with CENTCOM confirming two Navy destroyers entered the Gulf and two U.S. merchant ships transited the strait [Globe and Mail, France 24]. Iran warned the operation constitutes a ceasefire violation and claimed to have forced back a U.S. warship, though the Pentagon denied reports of missile strikes [Al Jazeera, Le Monde]. A South Korean ship suffered an explosion in the strait, with the cause under investigation [France 24]. In Europe, over 30 leaders convened in Yerevan for the European Political Community summit, with Armenia's hosting seen as a significant pivot away from Moscow [BBC, RFI]. President Macron called for coordinated diplomacy on Hormuz reopening, expressing skepticism about Washington's unilateral approach [RFI]. Meanwhile, India's BJP secured a historic breakthrough in West Bengal, one of its last major electoral frontiers [BBC, Nikkei Asia, Financial Times]. A suspected hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship off Cape Verde has killed three passengers, with Cape Verde authorities refusing to allow the vessel to dock [Guardian, BBC, WHO].

top_stories.headline: U.S. Navy Enters Persian Gulf to Break Iran Blockade as Tehran Warns of Escalation
bullets: U.S. Central Command confirmed two guided-missile destroyers entered the Gulf and two U.S.-flagged merchant vessels transited the Strait of Hormuz under 'Project Freedom'
Iran claimed it forced a U.S. warship to turn back and fired warning shots; CENTCOM denied reports that Iranian missiles struck any vessels
A South Korean ship carrying 24 crew suffered an explosion and fire in the strait; UAE intercepted three Iranian drones over its waters
Oil prices jumped 5% on initial reports before retreating; the strait normally carries one-fifth of global seaborne oil

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focused on military and commercial shipping details with balanced U.S.-Iran claims

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized live developments including South Korean ship incident and regional spillover

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Trump's 'all the cards' rhetoric and Iranian perspective on ceasefire violation

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Stressed Macron's skepticism about U.S. initiative and call for coordinated diplomacy

top_stories.headline: European Leaders Gather in Armenia for Summit as Host Nation Pivots from Russia
bullets: Over 30 European leaders plus Canadian PM Mark Carney convened in Yerevan for the European Political Community summit
Tuesday will see the first-ever bilateral EU-Armenia summit with von der Leyen and Costa attending
Armenia's shift follows Russia's failure to intervene during Azerbaijan's 2023 takeover of Nagorno-Karabakh
Putin recently reminded Armenia it receives Russian gas at $177.50 per 1,000 cubic meters versus $600 in Europe

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the symbolic significance of hosting given Armenia's traditional Russian alignment

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Focused on transatlantic tensions and Trump's shadow over proceedings

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Brief video coverage noting security and trade discussions

top_stories.headline: Modi's BJP Wins Historic Victory in West Bengal, Completing Eastern India Expansion
bullets: BJP is set to form government in West Bengal for the first time, defeating three-term incumbent Mamata Banerjee
The state has over 100 million voters, making the election comparable to a national poll in scale
BJP also won Assam for a third term; in Kerala, Congress-led coalition defeated the Left Front
In Tamil Nadu, actor-turned-politician Vijay's TVK party swept aside the ruling DMK government

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Provided extensive context on BJP's long struggle to win Bengal and broader political significance

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting on vote counting trends

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Emphasized end of left-wing governance in India and implications for opposition

top_stories.headline: Suspected Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship Kills Three; Vessel Barred from Cape Verde Port
bullets: Three passengers died (Dutch couple, German national) on MV Hondius; a British man is in intensive care in South Africa with confirmed hantavirus
The ship carrying 149 people from 23 countries is anchored off Cape Verde after authorities refused docking permission
WHO confirmed one laboratory case and five suspected cases; the virus typically spreads via rodent contact, rarely between humans
Ship was on polar expedition from Argentina to Antarctica before heading to Cape Verde

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Detailed timeline of deaths and company statements

sources.name: Guardian
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Cape Verde's refusal to allow docking and passenger accounts

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Provided background on hantavirus history including recent Gene Hackman family case

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Included passenger video testimony about uncertainty

sources.name: RNZ
sources.framing_note: Featured microbiologist explaining need to identify outbreak source

top_stories.headline: Ukrainian Drones Strike Moscow Residential Building Days Before Victory Day Parade
bullets: A drone hit an upmarket high-rise in southwest Moscow, less than 10km from the Kremlin, causing visible façade damage but no casualties
Russia reported intercepting 117 drones overnight across multiple regions; 60 targeted St. Petersburg in what governor called 'massive' attack
Vnukovo and Domodedovo airports suspended operations overnight
Attack came third consecutive night before Russia's scaled-back May 9 Victory Day parade

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting with geographic context and parade timing

additional_stories.headline: Two senior Republican lawmakers criticize Pentagon decision to cut 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany, saying it sends wrong signal to Russia
France uses G7 presidency to press for faster global methane cuts as IEA reports fossil fuel emissions remain near record highs
New Zealand exporters may be eligible for up to $1 billion in tariff refunds after U.S. Supreme Court reversed Trump's Liberation Day tariffs
Rudy Giuliani hospitalized in stable but critical condition; no details on illness released
Zambia cancels RightsCon human rights and technology summit days before start, citing need to align with 'national values'
Two U.S. service members missing after military exercises near cliff in southwestern Morocco
Jean-Luc Mélenchon announces candidacy for France's 2027 presidential election
Australia to wind back electric vehicle fringe benefits tax exemption starting 2027, saving $1.7 billion over four years
Global Sumud Flotilla carrying Gaza aid intercepted by Israel near Crete; New Zealanders among 175 detained
